# UC-13.1.4 · Splunk License Usage Trending, Pool Saturation, and Daily-Quota Burn Forecast (license_usage multisearch, rollover reconciliation, licenser warnings, slave reporting gaps, ingest actions and SmartStore caveats, workload-pricing visibility, SVC burn-down, end-of-day and end-of-month overage probability)

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Capacity, Configuration &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch shared usage like an apartment tower with one main fuse and smaller daytime allowances per flat on top of a bigger monthly bill. When one kitchen runs every appliance during a heat wave, the shared meter spins faster than hallway warnings can blink; we show which line is likely to trip first and what to unplug before dinner so neighbors keep power for the basics.*

---

## Description

Detects daily license-volume drift and per-pool saturation by fusing license_usage.log Usage detail with RolloverSummary and PoolUsageSummary reconciliation, splunkd_access licenser message warnings, LicenseUsage component slave-gap language, optional licenser groups REST hints, and inventory joins, then forecasting end-of-day and end-of-month overage probability using sliding burn-rate slopes, environment baselines, and severity-ranked routing.

## Value

Platform and finance leaders see a tamper-resistant, pool-level story that separates true ingestion growth from routing mistakes, reporting gaps, ingest-action toggles, and SmartStore operational noise while Splunk Cloud teams reconcile subscription units against raw gigabytes before warnings become violations.

## Implementation

Schedule platform_uc_13_1_4_license_pool_burn_forecast every five minutes with earliest=@d latest=now on a search head that reads license _internal, keep splunk_license_inventory.csv and cmdb splunk_license_pool_metadata pool keys aligned to the SPL coalesce ladder, tune critical and high thresholds after two calm baseline weeks, route critical rows to platform license on-call with Console drilldowns, and archive weekly CSV alert exports to a restricted platform_evidence index.

## Evidence

Saved search platform_uc_13_1_4_license_pool_burn_forecast with five-minute schedule, Monitoring Console license usage drilldowns, weekly CSV exports to platform_evidence index, and splunk_license_inventory.csv version hash archived with change tickets.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab license stack, misroute a synthetic high-volume sourcetype into a constrained pool under change control, confirm platform_uc_13_1_4_license_pool_burn_forecast returns critical or high within two five-minute windows with non-null recommended_action and rising projected_eod_pct.

### Negative scenario

After restoring correct routing and waiting for two slave reporting cadences with Monitoring Console showing healthy distance from soft caps, confirm severities return to low or medium and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the Splunk platform SREs who operate license masters, cluster managers, indexer fleets, search heads, and hybrid Splunk Cloud stacks, plus finance partners who translate raw volume into contractual commitments and subscription units. This use case isolates license metering, pool saturation, daily quota burn velocity, and forward-looking end-of-day and end-of-month overage probability narratives using fused internal telemetry rather than a single vanity chart. The license master maintains authoritative stacks and sub-stacks, assigns pools to peers and search heads, and receives usage reports from license slaves on a five-minute cadence in typical enterprise deployments. Slaves summarize indexed volume already normalized for licensing rules; the master rolls those summaries into stack-level accounting, warning thresholds, and violation posture. When slave reporting lags after maintenance, upgrades, or network partitions, the master can show a false sense of calm while bytes still accumulate on indexers, or conversely show warning storms while ingestion is already throttled—either outcome demands correlation across lanes, not a lone cumulative counter.

Understanding stacks versus pools is prerequisite reading before you tune severities. A license stack is the top-level container tied to a purchased entitlement; pools subdivide that stack for departmental chargeback, multi-tenant guardrails, or operational isolation between production and non-production workloads. Sub-stacks appear in complex enterprise contracts where trial capacity, disaster recovery entitlements, or burst packs ride alongside a primary stack. Misrouting a high-volume sourcetype into the wrong pool does not change physics on disk, but it changes who gets the bill and which warning counters increment first. Ingest actions on premises can discard or sample events before they hit the indexer pipeline, directly reducing metered bytes when configured correctly; disabling a fifty percent reduction rule mid-day can look like a rogue spike unless change metadata travels with the alert. SmartStore separates hot cache footprint from remote object storage economics: moving warm data to the remote tier changes storage spend and search behavior, but licensed indexing volume is driven by what passes through the metering pipeline rather than how many bytes later sit in an object store. Teams that mistake cache upload bursts for licensing explosions burn credibility with application owners.

Workload Pricing version two on Splunk Cloud introduces distinct lenses on ingest versus search workload, and the license_usage_summary style summaries help operators reconcile subscription units called SVCs against raw gigabytes for budgeting conversations. Splunk Enterprise remains predominantly gigabyte-framed for volume licenses, yet introspection and Monitoring Console views still matter for hybrid estates. The _introspection index and related host-level metrics expose CPU and pipeline pressure adjacent to license logs; they do not replace license_usage.log but explain why a pool suddenly accelerates when parsers change. Indexer acknowledgment channels confirm delivery from forwarders to indexing tier; acknowledgments speak to pipeline durability, not to license totals, and conflating the two derails incident reviews.

Federated search and hybrid search patterns can spread queries across remote providers without adding remote ingested volume to your local license tallies in the way newcomers sometimes fear; document that behavior for auditors so panic does not drive unnecessary purchase orders. The License Usage Report Builder remains the executive-friendly lens for stack and pool rollups; keep saved search outputs reconcilable with Report Builder exports quarterly. Enterprise license enforcement still orbits daily soft warnings, stack-level hard caps, and grace behaviors such as five violations in thirty days on volume licenses in many contracts—verify your exact paper with procurement rather than assuming defaults. Migration volume and migration preview modes shift how stacks absorb historical replays; tag DR and backfill windows in lookups so capacity reviews separate steady state from project work.

MV versus ML licensing modes change which features are available and how warnings present; maintain a column in splunk_license_inventory.csv that records mode per stack so eventstats baselines do not compare unlike estates. Certificate health on the license master remains a sibling concern covered elsewhere; this card still mentions that TLS trust loss between master and slaves blanks reporting without stopping ingestion, producing dangerous telemetry gaps. Legal and privacy reviewers should confirm pool names, business service labels, and owner teams are acceptable in alert bodies forwarded to chat systems.

Differentiation recap: this is license metering, pool saturation, subscription unit burn, and reporting-gap correlation—not universal forwarder tcpout health by itself, not search head cluster captain elections, not indexer replication factor compliance in isolation, and not disk fullness on a single mount without license context. It complements queue and pipeline cards by answering whether the bytes you are processing are about to breach contractual ceilings.

Risk briefing: partial-day gaps after a license master restart can depress counters until slaves catch up; do not declare victory solely because a chart dipped for twenty minutes. Sourcetype renames and data model migrations can re-bucket volume into new pools without a real traffic increase. Planned DR tests and synthetic backfills skew burn slopes; carry change_ticket_id into lookup rows or suppressions. Executive dashboards that only show daily totals miss slope; this control encodes slope explicitly with streamstats windows.

Collector posture: ensure license master and all slaves forward _internal license_usage.log without aggressive filtering; preserve type=Usage, type=RolloverSummary, and type=PoolUsageSummary lines. Capture splunkd_access entries for REST calls against licenser messages endpoints when security teams permit. LicenseUsage component lines on splunkd.log should remain available on the master for slave-gap language. For Splunk Cloud, schedule a low-frequency REST snapshot arm that a compatible role can execute, or mirror Cloud Monitoring Console exports into a summary index when REST from automation is disallowed.

RBAC: grant authors read access to _internal on license infrastructure, read access to cmdb splunk_license_pool_metadata if it exists, read access to splunk_license_inventory.csv, and restrict raw exports that embed sensitive host lists. Service accounts that run this search must not use interactive passwords on laptops.

Field normalization expectations: pool must join splunk_license_inventory.csv on a canonical lowercase key; if your estate labels pools with mixed case from the UI, normalize before joins. coalesce ladders absorb idx versus index, st versus sourcetype, and h versus host so multisearch arms remain compatible across minor versions. When cmdb metadata is missing, severities still fire but recommended_action should steer operators toward lookup hygiene.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary multisearch arms so a silent single lane cannot hide a genuine license crisis. Arm one reads index=_internal source=*license_usage.log with type=Usage, preserving pool, idx, st, h, and byte counters your build exposes as b or bytes. Tune props.conf so truncation does not strip long pool identifiers. Arm two reads the same file family for type=RolloverSummary and type=PoolUsageSummary to reconcile end-of-day rollups against incremental Usage sums; mismatches between arms one and two often reveal clock skew, delayed slave batches, or manual pool reassignment during maintenance. Arm three reads index=_internal source=*splunkd_access.log for uri paths matching licenser messages; match WARN_OVER_DAILY_QUOTA and WARN_OVER_LICENSE_STACK_QUOTA substrings in _raw to populate license_warning_count style aggregates. Arm four reads index=_internal source=*splunkd.log with component=LicenseUsage for narratives about slave reporting, stack moves, and quota language that contextualize sudden jumps without immediate indexer queue collapse. Arm five issues a constrained rest call to /services/server/licenser/groups against splunk_server=local on hosts where automation permits, capturing group titles or identifiers and effective quota hints for Splunk Cloud subscription conversations; when REST is banned from scheduled searches, replace this arm with a summary index fed by an approved external poller using the same schema.

Maintain splunk_license_inventory.csv with at least pool, owner_team, business_service, daily_quota_gb, monthly_committed_svc, environment, and runbook_url. The daily_quota_gb column should reflect the contractual daily soft cap used for percentage math, not marketing rounded numbers. monthly_committed_svc should record Splunk Cloud subscription units or an estate-agreed monthly byte commitment expressed in comparable gigabyte-equivalent units your finance partners approve; document the unit in the lookup README so monthly_burn_pct is never misread as a fraction of an unrelated ledger. Populate index=cmdb sourcetype=splunk_license_pool_metadata with pool_tier and cost_center fields for finance joins; refresh at least weekly.

Ingest actions belong in serverclass documentation: operators must know which indexes use discard, filter, or routing rules that alter metered volume, and those rules must appear in change tickets when toggled. SmartStore documentation should clarify that remote upload activity is a storage and search latency concern first; attribute licensing spikes to on-indexer metering unless support confirms a known interaction in your exact release train.

Monitoring Console posture: validate License Usage views still render for the same stacks this search monitors; when UI totals diverge from _internal sums, suspect search affinity, partial peer sampling, or role filters before muting alerts.

Example CLI helpers for design reviews:

```bash
splunk list licenser-locals --no-prompt
splunk list licenser-pools --no-prompt
curl -k -u svc:token https://license-master:8089/services/licenser/messages?output_mode=json | head
```

Pre-save validation: index=_internal source=*license_usage.log type=Usage earliest=-1h must return events on active stacks; absence implies collection failure, not zero usage. splunkd_access licenser messages arm should return sporadic rows on busy stacks; total absence may mean URI filtering or HEC routing gaps.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as platform_uc_13_1_4_license_pool_burn_forecast with a five-minute schedule during steady production, dispatch.earliest_time=@d, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after local tuning. Throttle duplicate pool rows for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from high to critical. Include owner_team, business_service, and recommended_action tokens in pager bodies so responders open the correct runbook without rerunning five ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans Usage detail, Rollover and Pool summaries, licenser REST access warnings, LicenseUsage component narratives, and optional licenser groups REST snapshots so correlation survives single-lane dropouts. streamstats builds a running cumulative sum of usage_detail volume per pool, then applies a sliding window linear slope estimate of bytes per second converted to bytes per minute for burn rate. eventstats averages bytes_today by environment for fleet context dashboards. case maps combined daily_used_pct, projected_eod_pct, license_warning_count, and pool_overflow_count into severities with recommended_action tokens. join pulls splunk_license_inventory.csv and left joins cmdb splunk_license_pool_metadata on pool. coalesce guards missing pool keys. The closing table preserves analyst-ready columns for executive and capacity reviews.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.4 License usage trending pool saturation daily-quota burn forecast workload-pricing SVC visibility multisearch license_usage rollover licenser messages LicenseUsage rest groups. Tunables earliest=@d latest=now crit_daily_pct=95 crit_proj_eod_pct=110 warn_msg_sum=5")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal source=*license_usage.log type=Usage earliest=@d latest=now
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pool,pool_id,stack,"",""))))
      | eval idx=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(idx,index,"",""))))
      | eval st=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(st,sourcetype,"",""))))
      | eval h=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(h,host,splunk_server,"",""))))
      | eval vol=tonumber(coalesce(b,bytes,vol,0))
      | eval lane="usage_detail"
      | fields _time pool idx st h vol lane ]
    [ search index=_internal source=*license_usage.log (type=RolloverSummary OR type=PoolUsageSummary) earliest=@d latest=now
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pool,pool_id,stack,"",""))))
      | eval vol=tonumber(coalesce(b,bytes,indexed_volume,0))
      | eval lane="rollover_pool"
      | fields _time pool vol lane ]
    [ search index=_internal source=*splunkd_access.log uri="*licenser/messages*" earliest=@d latest=now
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pool,query_pool,"stack_wide"))))
      | eval msg_hit=if(match(_raw,"WARN_OVER_DAILY_QUOTA|WARN_OVER_LICENSE_STACK_QUOTA"),1,0)
      | eval lane="lic_messages"
      | fields _time pool msg_hit lane ]
    [ search index=_internal source=*splunkd.log component=LicenseUsage earliest=@d latest=now
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pool,stack_label,"pool_unknown"))))
      | eval gap_like=if(match(lower(_raw),"slave|report|latency|reassign|stack|quota"),1,0)
      | eval lane="lic_slavedrift"
      | fields _time pool gap_like lane ]
    [ | rest /services/server/licenser/groups splunk_server=local count=0
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(title,id,name,"cloud_lic_group"))))
      | eval svc_effective=tonumber(coalesce(effective_quota,quota,0))
      | eval lane="svc_rest"
      | fields pool svc_effective lane ]
| eval pool=lower(trim(coalesce(pool,"unknown_pool")))
| eval vol_f=if(lane=="usage_detail",vol,0)
| eval warn_hit=if(lane=="lic_messages",coalesce(msg_hit,0),0)
| eval gap_hit=if(lane=="lic_slavedrift",coalesce(gap_like,0),0)
| sort 0 pool + _time
| streamstats global=f sum(vol_f) AS bytes_cum BY pool
| streamstats window=15 current=t global=f first(_time) AS win_t0 last(_time) AS win_t1 first(bytes_cum) AS bc0 last(bytes_cum) AS bc1 BY pool
| eval slope_bps=if((win_t1-win_t0)>0 AND bc1>=bc0,(bc1-bc0)/(win_t1-win_t0),null())
| eval daily_burn_rate_bytes_per_min=round(coalesce(slope_bps*60,0),8)
| stats max(_time) AS _time max(bytes_cum) AS bytes_today max(daily_burn_rate_bytes_per_min) AS burn_bpm sum(warn_hit) AS license_warning_count sum(gap_hit) AS pool_overflow_count max(eval(if(lane=="svc_rest",svc_effective,0))) AS svc_quota_hint max(eval(if(lane=="rollover_pool",vol,null()))) AS rollover_reconcile_bytes BY pool
| eval burn_bpm=coalesce(burn_bpm,0)
| eval daily_burn_rate_gb_per_min=round(burn_bpm/1024/1024/1024,8)
| join type=left max=0 pool
    [| inputlookup splunk_license_inventory.csv
     | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(pool)))
     | fields pool owner_team business_service daily_quota_gb monthly_committed_svc environment runbook_url ]
| join type=left max=0 pool
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=splunk_license_pool_metadata earliest=-7d@d latest=now
     | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(pool)))
     | dedup pool
     | fields pool pool_tier cost_center ]
| eval daily_quota_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(daily_quota_gb,0))*1024*1024*1024
| eval daily_used_gb=round(bytes_today/1024/1024/1024,4)
| eval daily_used_pct=if(daily_quota_bytes>0,round(100*bytes_today/daily_quota_bytes,2),null())
| eval eod_epoch=relative_time(now(),"+1d@d")
| eval minutes_to_eod=round((eod_epoch-now())/60,4)
| eval projected_eod_bytes=bytes_today + coalesce(burn_bpm,0)*minutes_to_eod
| eval projected_eod_gb=round(projected_eod_bytes/1024/1024/1024,4)
| eval projected_eod_pct=if(daily_quota_bytes>0,round(100*projected_eod_bytes/daily_quota_bytes,2),null())
| eval monthly_commit_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(monthly_committed_svc,0))*1024*1024*1024
| eval monthly_burn_pct=if(monthly_commit_bytes>0,round(100*bytes_today/monthly_commit_bytes,2),null())
| eventstats avg(bytes_today) AS env_avg_bytes_today BY environment
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(daily_used_pct,0)>95 OR coalesce(projected_eod_pct,0)>110 OR coalesce(license_warning_count,0)>=5,"critical",
    coalesce(daily_used_pct,0)>85 OR coalesce(projected_eod_pct,0)>100 OR coalesce(license_warning_count,0)>=3,"high",
    coalesce(daily_used_pct,0)>70 OR coalesce(projected_eod_pct,0)>92 OR coalesce(pool_overflow_count,0)>=8,"medium",
    true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    coalesce(license_warning_count,0)>=5,"open_license_master_health_review_throttle_ingest_reroute_pools_validate_slave_reporting_cadence",
    coalesce(projected_eod_pct,0)>110,"execute_ingest_actions_reduce_volume_shift_sourcetypes_rebalance_pools_before_daily_rollover",
    coalesce(daily_used_pct,0)>95,"emergency_capacity_review_disable_debug_loggers_validate_smartstore_attribution_not_double_counted",
    coalesce(pool_overflow_count,0)>=8,"investigate_license_slave_gap_after_upgrade_correlate_license_usage_log_latency",
    true(),"continue_monitoring_reconcile_license_usage_report_builder_with_internal_meters")
| table _time pool owner_team business_service environment daily_quota_gb daily_used_gb daily_used_pct projected_eod_gb projected_eod_pct daily_burn_rate_gb_per_min monthly_burn_pct license_warning_count severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[platform_uc_13_1_4_license_pool_burn_forecast_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = @d
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_1_4_license_pool_burn_forecast | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch skew, materialize each arm hourly into a summary index keyed on pool and lane, widen operational alerts to earliest=-2h@h on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. Cap REST concurrency on license masters during incidents so diagnostics do not amplify control-plane load.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the license master. Compare Monitoring Console license usage totals to the latest platform_uc_13_1_4_license_pool_burn_forecast rows for the same pools and stacks. When totals diverge, verify that the search head executing the saved search can read all relevant _internal peers, that time zones align on day boundaries, and that partial maintenance windows did not truncate license_usage.log forwarding.

Reconcile Usage sums against RolloverSummary and PoolUsageSummary arms for the same UTC day. Persistent gaps indicate slave batch delays, master restart effects, or manual pool edits; pull contemporaneous LicenseUsage lines for narrative confirmation.

Validate licenser messages hits by manually requesting licenser messages endpoints during a controlled test window with governance approval; ensure WARN strings in _raw match the substring logic in the SPL before expanding alert distribution lists.

For Splunk Cloud workload pricing, compare svc_quota_hint snapshots with tenant admin exports for subscription units; mismatches often reflect automation running on the wrong member or stale tokens.

Synthetic validation: in a lab stack, temporarily misroute a synthetic high-volume sourcetype into a small test pool under change control and confirm severity escalates within two five-minute windows, then revert routing and confirm decay.

Negative test: on a calm day without backfill, confirm filtering severity to critical and high returns zero rows for two hours while Monitoring Console shows healthy distance from warning thresholds.

Job Inspector: confirm scanned volumes remain proportional to _internal license file sizes; explosions suggest unbounded wildcards in supporting macros.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Misrouted high-volume sourcetype lands in the wrong pool: revert outputs or transforms routing, rebalance volume using supported pool reassignment workflows, validate Usage lines move to the intended pool within two reporting cadences, and document finance impact with owner_team sign-off.

Case 2 — SmartStore cache upload spike misread as licensing explosion: correlate object store latency with search behavior, confirm metering arm shows usage_detail stability while storage dashboards spike, and educate stakeholders that cache churn differs from licensed ingest except where documented for your release.

Case 3 — Ingest-actions reduce-by-fifty-percent rule disabled mid-day: replay change tickets, re-enable or replace with Edge Processor rules, validate metered bytes fall after lag expectations, and tune alert suppression metadata for the maintenance window.

Case 4 — Rogue debug logger emits full HTTP bodies: identify the sourcetype and host class, disable verbose transforms, redact at source, and confirm daily_used_pct falls without touching legitimate security telemetry.

Case 5 — License-warning daily counter climbs while ingestion looks flat: inspect licenser messages arm for WARN_OVER strings, validate daily soft caps versus true stack totals, and open a license master health review before violations accumulate.

Case 6 — Cluster-manager license-slave drift after upgrade: compare LicenseUsage gap_like density with upgrade tickets, restart slave reporting in vendor-supported order, validate five-minute cadence resumes, and reconcile rollover summaries.

Case 7 — Splunk Cloud SVC over-allocation alert: align finance subscription workbook with svc_rest arm snapshots, adjust workload pricing rules only after platform architecture review, and avoid confusing search workload signals with ingest meters.

Case 8 — Index data inflation from props.conf misclassification: sourcetype demotion or rename expands event sizes; diff recent cluster bundles, restore prior parsing, validate st-level Usage returns to baseline.

Case 9 — Partial-day gap from license master restart: pause paging during documented restart windows, require dual-source confirmation from RolloverSummary once catch-up completes, and annotate lookups with restart metadata.

Case 10 — Sourcetype rename causes pool re-bucket: update inventory mappings, adjust chargeback dashboards, and confirm warnings reflect intentional moves rather than stealth growth.

Case 11 — Planned ingest spike during DR test: gate alerts with change metadata, compare slopes against approved runbook envelopes, and separate DR labels in monthly reviews.

Case 12 — Backfill load skews daily projection: switch alert clone to median slope or exclude backfill hosts using lookup flags, validate projected_eod_pct after excluding known replay windows.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, inputlookup, and join appear; final table lists fifteen analyst columns including pool, owner_team, business_service, environment, daily_quota_gb, daily_used_gb, daily_used_pct, projected_eod_gb, projected_eod_pct, daily_burn_rate_gb_per_min, monthly_burn_pct, license_warning_count, severity, and recommended_action.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.4 License usage trending pool saturation daily-quota burn forecast workload-pricing SVC visibility multisearch license_usage rollover licenser messages LicenseUsage rest groups. Tunables earliest=@d latest=now crit_daily_pct=95 crit_proj_eod_pct=110 warn_msg_sum=5")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal source=*license_usage.log type=Usage earliest=@d latest=now
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pool,pool_id,stack,"",""))))
      | eval idx=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(idx,index,"",""))))
      | eval st=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(st,sourcetype,"",""))))
      | eval h=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(h,host,splunk_server,"",""))))
      | eval vol=tonumber(coalesce(b,bytes,vol,0))
      | eval lane="usage_detail"
      | fields _time pool idx st h vol lane ]
    [ search index=_internal source=*license_usage.log (type=RolloverSummary OR type=PoolUsageSummary) earliest=@d latest=now
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pool,pool_id,stack,"",""))))
      | eval vol=tonumber(coalesce(b,bytes,indexed_volume,0))
      | eval lane="rollover_pool"
      | fields _time pool vol lane ]
    [ search index=_internal source=*splunkd_access.log uri="*licenser/messages*" earliest=@d latest=now
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pool,query_pool,"stack_wide"))))
      | eval msg_hit=if(match(_raw,"WARN_OVER_DAILY_QUOTA|WARN_OVER_LICENSE_STACK_QUOTA"),1,0)
      | eval lane="lic_messages"
      | fields _time pool msg_hit lane ]
    [ search index=_internal source=*splunkd.log component=LicenseUsage earliest=@d latest=now
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pool,stack_label,"pool_unknown"))))
      | eval gap_like=if(match(lower(_raw),"slave|report|latency|reassign|stack|quota"),1,0)
      | eval lane="lic_slavedrift"
      | fields _time pool gap_like lane ]
    [ | rest /services/server/licenser/groups splunk_server=local count=0
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(title,id,name,"cloud_lic_group"))))
      | eval svc_effective=tonumber(coalesce(effective_quota,quota,0))
      | eval lane="svc_rest"
      | fields pool svc_effective lane ]
| eval pool=lower(trim(coalesce(pool,"unknown_pool")))
| eval vol_f=if(lane=="usage_detail",vol,0)
| eval warn_hit=if(lane=="lic_messages",coalesce(msg_hit,0),0)
| eval gap_hit=if(lane=="lic_slavedrift",coalesce(gap_like,0),0)
| sort 0 pool + _time
| streamstats global=f sum(vol_f) AS bytes_cum BY pool
| streamstats window=15 current=t global=f first(_time) AS win_t0 last(_time) AS win_t1 first(bytes_cum) AS bc0 last(bytes_cum) AS bc1 BY pool
| eval slope_bps=if((win_t1-win_t0)>0 AND bc1>=bc0,(bc1-bc0)/(win_t1-win_t0),null())
| eval daily_burn_rate_bytes_per_min=round(coalesce(slope_bps*60,0),8)
| stats max(_time) AS _time max(bytes_cum) AS bytes_today max(daily_burn_rate_bytes_per_min) AS burn_bpm sum(warn_hit) AS license_warning_count sum(gap_hit) AS pool_overflow_count max(eval(if(lane=="svc_rest",svc_effective,0))) AS svc_quota_hint max(eval(if(lane=="rollover_pool",vol,null()))) AS rollover_reconcile_bytes BY pool
| eval burn_bpm=coalesce(burn_bpm,0)
| eval daily_burn_rate_gb_per_min=round(burn_bpm/1024/1024/1024,8)
| join type=left max=0 pool
    [| inputlookup splunk_license_inventory.csv
     | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(pool)))
     | fields pool owner_team business_service daily_quota_gb monthly_committed_svc environment runbook_url ]
| join type=left max=0 pool
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=splunk_license_pool_metadata earliest=-7d@d latest=now
     | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(pool)))
     | dedup pool
     | fields pool pool_tier cost_center ]
| eval daily_quota_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(daily_quota_gb,0))*1024*1024*1024
| eval daily_used_gb=round(bytes_today/1024/1024/1024,4)
| eval daily_used_pct=if(daily_quota_bytes>0,round(100*bytes_today/daily_quota_bytes,2),null())
| eval eod_epoch=relative_time(now(),"+1d@d")
| eval minutes_to_eod=round((eod_epoch-now())/60,4)
| eval projected_eod_bytes=bytes_today + coalesce(burn_bpm,0)*minutes_to_eod
| eval projected_eod_gb=round(projected_eod_bytes/1024/1024/1024,4)
| eval projected_eod_pct=if(daily_quota_bytes>0,round(100*projected_eod_bytes/daily_quota_bytes,2),null())
| eval monthly_commit_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(monthly_committed_svc,0))*1024*1024*1024
| eval monthly_burn_pct=if(monthly_commit_bytes>0,round(100*bytes_today/monthly_commit_bytes,2),null())
| eventstats avg(bytes_today) AS env_avg_bytes_today BY environment
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(daily_used_pct,0)>95 OR coalesce(projected_eod_pct,0)>110 OR coalesce(license_warning_count,0)>=5,"critical",
    coalesce(daily_used_pct,0)>85 OR coalesce(projected_eod_pct,0)>100 OR coalesce(license_warning_count,0)>=3,"high",
    coalesce(daily_used_pct,0)>70 OR coalesce(projected_eod_pct,0)>92 OR coalesce(pool_overflow_count,0)>=8,"medium",
    true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    coalesce(license_warning_count,0)>=5,"open_license_master_health_review_throttle_ingest_reroute_pools_validate_slave_reporting_cadence",
    coalesce(projected_eod_pct,0)>110,"execute_ingest_actions_reduce_volume_shift_sourcetypes_rebalance_pools_before_daily_rollover",
    coalesce(daily_used_pct,0)>95,"emergency_capacity_review_disable_debug_loggers_validate_smartstore_attribution_not_double_counted",
    coalesce(pool_overflow_count,0)>=8,"investigate_license_slave_gap_after_upgrade_correlate_license_usage_log_latency",
    true(),"continue_monitoring_reconcile_license_usage_report_builder_with_internal_meters")
| table _time pool owner_team business_service environment daily_quota_gb daily_used_gb daily_used_pct projected_eod_gb projected_eod_pct daily_burn_rate_gb_per_min monthly_burn_pct license_warning_count severity recommended_action
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_pct) AS cpu_pct latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS lic_host
| join type=left max=0 lic_host [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Inventory.device_id) AS device_id FROM datamodel=Inventory WHERE nodename=Inventory.Inv_Device earliest=-7d@d latest=now BY Inventory.hostname
| rename Inventory.hostname AS lic_host ]
| eval lic_host=lower(trim(lic_host))
| table lic_host cpu_pct mem_used device_id
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by pool with sparklines of daily_used_pct and projected_eod_pct; single-value tiles for license_warning_count; dual-axis chart of bytes_today versus env_avg_bytes_today; drilldowns to license_usage.log Usage lines, RolloverSummary events, and splunkd_access licenser messages.

## Known False Positives

Scheduled disaster-recovery backfill windows replay historical bytes and can push projected end-of-day percentages above polite thresholds even when live traffic is calm; require change-calendar correlation before finance escalations. Planned bulk imports and migration projects inflate Usage slopes for hours; tag those hosts in inventory or route alerts to the data-onboarding queue instead of the platform incident bridge. License stack tier changes and entitlement renewals move daily quota goalposts mid-month; refresh splunk_license_inventory.csv the same day contracts change or suffer false criticals. Weekly maintenance windows that restart license masters or peers create short reporting gaps and suppressed counters; pair rollover summaries with Maintenance metadata before paging application owners. Development and test instances that share a stack name with production in poorly curated lookups can absorb synthetic load and look like production saturation; enforce environment labels and separate pool keys. Executive summaries that only glance at absolute gigabytes miss pool reassignment noise; keep percentage and slope columns attached so false calm does not survive leadership review.
